About
"Born to a military father and a businesswoman. Fuelled by a passion for bridging worlds."
I grew up in Chattogram, Bangladesh, left at 18 for London, and went from there to Canberra. Each move added a layer. Every course, every job, every conversation I was in the room for shaped how I think about power, development, and the people caught between them.

Right now I am an Associate Researcher at RAPID, one of Bangladesh's leading think tanks, doing geopolitical and geoeconomic research that connects to real policy decisions.
CFB · Dhaka ANU · 2023 Westminster · 2019 Bangladesh IR · Policy Peace & Conflict

Academic Work

Research & Writing.

01
Media vs. Academics: Analysing Different Approaches on the Reporting of Terrorism
A comparative paper on how mass media and academia each approach terrorism. Covers the competitive business logic driving inaccurate reporting, case studies from the 1985 Oklahoma bombing to the 2013 Boston Marathon coverage, and Schmid's definitional framework for classifying acts of terror.
POLS 8027TerrorismMedia AnalysisPolicy
Read paper ↗
POLS 8027
02
Reintegrating Survivors of Sexual Violence: Bangladesh and the 1971 Liberation War
A case study analysis of the Birangona, survivors of systematic sexual violence during the 1971 Liberation War. Examines reintegration barriers through a gendered lens: stigma, economic exclusion, psychological trauma, and draws parallels with comfort women in South Korean colonial memory.
INTR 8064Gender & JusticeBangladesh 1971Conflict Studies
Read paper ↗
INTR 8064
03
Bangladesh and the World Bank: The Case of the Padma Multipurpose Bridge (2012 to 2022)
A decade-long country case study on a relationship defined by a corruption allegation that was never proven in court. Bangladesh withdrew WB funding, self-financed the $3.9B bridge, and became the bank's largest IDA program globally within a decade. Analyses discourse shifts and the overuse of GDP as a development indicator.
ANTH 8107Global GovernanceDevelopment PolicyBangladesh
Read paper ↗
ANTH 8107
04
Do Past Global Injustices Generate Global Obligations of Reparation?
An ethics essay on colonial reparations through the British colonisation of the Indian subcontinent and Africa. Covers imposed governance, the divide-and-rule partition, cultural destruction, resource extraction, and neo-colonial dependency. Argues for a holistic, multi-dimensional approach against a backdrop of legal and political resistance from former colonial powers.
INTR 8080Global EthicsColonialismReparations
Read paper ↗
INTR 8080
05
Associate Researcher at RAPID Think Tank, Dhaka
Research on geopolitical and geoeconomic issues at Research and Policy Integration for Development. Preparing policy briefs and reports for evidence-based decision-making, contributing to sustainable development strategy through stakeholder engagement and rigorous analysis.
2024 — 2025Policy ResearchDhakaDevelopment
RAPIDDhaka · 2024–2025
Professional History

Where I’ve Worked.

Aug 2025 — Present
Centre for Business (CFB), Chittagong
Bangladesh
Senior Research Associate
  • Lead a department examining how global affairs shape the business environment in Bangladesh
  • Research sits at the intersection of international developments and their real impact on trade, policy, and economic activity in the country
  • Work used by businesses and stakeholders navigating an increasingly interconnected world
Nov 2024 — Jul 2025
Research and Policy Integration for Development (RAPID)
Dhaka, Bangladesh
Associate Researcher
  • In-depth research on geopolitical and geoeconomic issues, with focus on implications for developing states
  • Policy briefs, reports, and presentations for government and institutional clients on sustainable development strategy
  • Stakeholder engagement and contribution to RAPID's evidence-based decision-making mission
Aug 2024 — Present
La Aristocracy · The Flour Bloom · Eat & Treat
Dhaka, Bangladesh
Head of Operations

Bangladesh · England · Australia

The Journey.

Drag to explore
01
Bangladesh · Origin
Where it started.
Born and raised in Chattogram to a military father and a businesswoman mother. Completed O and A Levels before setting off abroad. Bangladesh is in everything I research.
02
London · England · 2019
University of Westminster.
Graduated with a B.A. in International Relations and Development. Three years in London. Left knowing the world differently.
03
Australia · 2022 to 2023
Australian National University.
M.A. International Relations, specialising in Peace and Conflict Studies. PARSA Coursework Officer for the College of Asia Pacific.
04
Australia · July 2023
Master of International Relations.
Awarded July 2023. Returned to Dhaka shortly after.
05
Australia · Canberra
Life in Canberra.
Two years in Australia's capital. Museums, galleries, politics, and a lot of cold mornings. The city is quieter than most expect and better for it.
06
Australia · Victoria
Great Ocean Road.
Victoria. The rock formations, the wind, the grey water. Saw it on a day trip and it stayed.
